name: ICR
title: Integrated Campaign Registry (ICR) Implementation Guide
publisher: UNICEF Integrated Campaign Registry project (Ona + Crosscut)
description: HL7 FHIR R4 Implementation Guide for UNICEF's Integrated Campaign Registry (ICR): a standards framework that lets public health campaigns (immunization SIAs, polio, NTD mass drug administration, malaria ITN/IRS, vitamin A) share, exchange, and reuse campaign data and metadata instead of re-collecting it every round. CarePlan is the architectural keystone: PlanDefinition carries the reusable campaign protocol, CarePlan the campaign execution, Task the operational unit of work, Group the households and target populations, and Location the administrative hierarchy and geospatial identity (Overture Maps GERS).

resource.name: ICR Campaign Type
resource.description: The type of public health campaign, grouped by delivery model rather than disease (working doc §3).

resource.name: Campaign Round
resource.description: Round number of this campaign execution. Each round is its own CarePlan instantiating the same protocol, linked to an umbrella campaign via CarePlan.partOf (working doc §6.3).

resource.name: ICR Campaign Type
resource.description: Campaign types by delivery model. Binding: required on ICRCampaignProtocol.type and ICRCampaign.category.
